branchTraceDecision.reason: fix/ branches are trace-required under the branch-based trace policy.

evidenceBoundary.summary: This trace records observable cleanup work after the DaaS account correction had already been applied to preview and production D1, and the subsequent branch-prefix trace governance change.

trigger.summary: PR #250 corrected the DaaS account data in both preview and production. The repository still contained temporary SQL migrations and deploy workflow hooks that should not become permanent fixtures. While correcting trace coverage, the branch trace posture also needed to change so traces are driven by branch prefix rather than by the user typing [reasoning].
observedIssues: The auth data correction migration remained under infra/cloudflare/migrations.
The preview-only data correction migration remained under infra/cloudflare/migrations/preview.
The deploy workflow still referenced and applied those temporary correction files.
Route-state tests still asserted that the temporary correction files existed.
Trace coverage relied on user prompt posture rather than branch posture.
Unapproved branch prefixes such as claude/ were not explicitly blocked by the trace coverage contract.

changes: Removed AUTH_DATA_CORRECTION_MIGRATION from workflow environment variables.
Removed PREVIEW_RESEARCH_OPERATIONS_DAAS_CORRECTION from workflow environment variables.
Removed the production D1 execute step for the temporary auth correction migration.
Removed the preview D1 execute step for the temporary preview correction migration.
Deleted the one-off production/auth correction migration.
Deleted the one-off preview correction migration.
Removed file reads for the deleted correction migrations.
Removed assertions requiring deploy workflow correction variables.
Removed assertions requiring the deleted SQL files and their audit event names.
Changed trace posture from user-token driven to branch-prefix driven.
Declared feature/, chore/, test/, fix/ and perf/ as trace-required branch prefixes.
Declared hotfix/ as trace-exempt.
Declared the approved work-branch prefixes and prohibited unapproved prefixes such as claude/.
Added branch prefix normalisation for GitHub Actions and local branch names.
Added explicit allowed branch prefix list.
Added explicit trace-required prefix list.
Blocked unapproved prefixes such as claude/, codex/, bugfix/ and experiment/.
Skipped trace coverage for hotfix/ branches.
Added route-state style tests for trace-required, trace-exempt and invalid-prefix cases.
Added branch governance to the GitHub bundle doctrine and mandatory sequence.
Recorded the branch trace posture in the GitHub bundle changelog.
Recorded the repeatable repository learning in RECENT_LEARNINGS.md.

validation.initialFailure: CI and Release Gate failed because the 2026-05-14 trace directory was missing.
validation.firstFix: Added a JSON trace under docs/agent-audit/reasoning/2026/05/14.
validation.policyUpdate: Changed trace coverage so branches, not the [reasoning] token alone, determine trace coverage requirements.
validation.expectedResult: The agent trace coverage test should find today's trace directory and at least one JSON trace artefact, and branch-prefix tests should enforce approved branch classes.

residualRisks: This cleanup does not change D1 data. It only removes the temporary mechanism that applied the already-completed correction.
If the DaaS account data is ever lost again, it should be corrected through a deliberate operational data fix rather than by reintroducing these temporary migrations.
The branch prefix policy is enforced through repository trace coverage and tests. It should also be reflected in GitHub branch rulesets where available.
